name: Behaviour-Driven Development
def: Specifying behaviour in business-readable Given-When-Then scenarios that double as automated tests.
problem: Align developers, QA and business on behaviour using a shared, executable language.
mechanism: Scenarios written in Gherkin (Given/When/Then) map to step definitions that drive the system, serving as living, executable specifications.
limits: Easy to write brittle UI scenarios
failures: Scenarios that are just translated unit tests
whenNot: Pure technical units with no business-facing behaviour
myths: "BDD is just testing with Gherkin" — it is primarily a collaboration/specification practice
